meta.note: Untried Asia/Caucasus sweep: Myanmar, Afghanistan, Yemen, Palestine, Syria. Ban-adds via existing_book_id: I Am Malala (AF), A Banquet for Seaweed (PS + SY), Speak Bird Speak Again (PS), The Silence and the Roar (SY). Palestine (PS) added as a new country (PA + Hamas authorities; Israeli restrictions tracked separately). Held: Death Is Hard Work (SY, the 'banned in Syria' line actually refers to In Praise of Hatred, already held), Iraq (only category-level/author-persecution, no new title), and various reputation-only controversies.

entries.title: Smile as They Bow
entries.description_ban: Novel set among nat-kadaw spirit-medium performers; the Press Scrutiny Board held a redacted version for ~12 years, decrying its gender/sexuality themes as an offence to cultural values.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific censorship-board ban; documented by Five Books.

entries.title: Myanmar's Rohingya Genocide: Identity, History and Hate Speech
entries.description_ban: The junta banned the book and revoked the distributor's licence under the 1962 Printing & Publishing Law, citing 'harm to an ethnic group'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific junta ban; documented by Radio Free Asia.

entries.title: My Possessive Step-Bro
entries.description_ban: Reported as the first LGBTQ+-themed book banned after the 2021 coup, deemed obscene under the Printing & Publication Law.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific junta ban; documented by DVB.

entries.title: The World of the Romancebots
entries.description_ban: Pulled from shelves by the publisher after the Printing & Publication Registration Department flagged it as 'obscene material' and the Ministry of Information threatened legal action.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific withdrawal under government pressure; documented by DVB.

entries.title: A Butterfly Rests on My Heart
entries.description_ban: One of seven LGBTQ+ titles banned by the Ministry of Information in January 2025 as 'obscene' and 'not accepted by Myanmar society'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Radio Free Asia.

entries.title: 1500 Miles to You
entries.description_ban: Among the seven LGBTQ+ books banned by the Ministry of Information in January 2025 as obscene.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Radio Free Asia.

entries.title: Love Planted by Hate
entries.description_ban: Among the seven LGBTQ+ books banned by the Ministry of Information in January 2025 as obscene.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Radio Free Asia.

entries.title: Tie the Knot of Love
entries.description_ban: Among the seven LGBTQ+ books banned by the Ministry of Information in January 2025 as obscene.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Radio Free Asia.

entries.title: Match Made in Clouds
entries.description_ban: Among the seven LGBTQ+ books banned by the Ministry of Information in January 2025 as obscene.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Radio Free Asia.

entries.title: DISO+Extra
entries.description_ban: Among the seven LGBTQ+ books banned by the Ministry of Information in January 2025 as obscene.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by Radio Free Asia.

entries.title: Jesus the Son of Man
entries.description_ban: Named on the Taliban Ministry of Information & Culture's ~400-title banned list for 'blasphemous expressions' and removed from Afghan bookshops.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban ban; documented by Dawn/AFP.

entries.title: Twilight of the Eastern Gods
entries.description_ban: The Albanian novel was named on the Taliban's banned list and pulled from circulation.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban ban; documented by Dawn/AFP.

entries.title: Afghanistan and the Region: A West Asian Perspective
entries.description_ban: Flagged by Taliban censors for 'negative propaganda' and removed from shelves.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban ban; documented by Dawn/AFP.

entries.title: The History of Jihadi Groups in Afghanistan
entries.description_ban: Named among titles removed from Afghan bookshops under the Taliban's 'un-Islamic / anti-government' sweep.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban ban; documented by Dawn/AFP (medium confidence).

entries.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
entries.description_ban: The Western bestseller was named on the Taliban's ~400-title ban list (as un-Islamic) and disappeared from Kabul shelves.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban ban; documented by Al Arabiya/AFP.

entries.title: Brave, Not Perfect
entries.description_ban: Listed among titles by women authors on the October 2024 Taliban ban list distributed to booksellers.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban ban; documented by Al Arabiya/AFP.

entries.title: Waziristan: The Last Refuge
entries.description_ban: The author told RFE/RL and VOA his regional history was banned by Taliban censors who 'banned them just by looking at the titles'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban ban; documented by RFE/RL.

entries.title: Political Terminology and International Relations
entries.description_ban: Pulled from university curricula in the 2025 higher-education purge of women authors as 'anti-Sharia'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Taliban curriculum ban; documented by Al Jazeera.

entries.title: I Am Malala
entries.description_ban: Named among the ~400 titles distributed to Afghan booksellers as banned for being against 'Islamic principles and national interest'. Adds an Afghanistan ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds an Afghanistan ban; documented by VOA.

entries.title: Mountain Boats
entries.description_ban: Novel (Qawarib jabaliyya) confiscated by the Yemeni Ministry of Culture for 'insulting morality, religion, and the conventions of Yemeni society'; criminal charges and an extremist campaign drove al-Ahdal into exile until an international intervention led by Günter Grass.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry confiscation; documented via Wikipedia (cited to Comma Press).

entries.title: Crime in Ramallah
entries.description_ban: The Palestinian Authority's Attorney General ordered all copies confiscated from West Bank bookshops in February 2017 for 'indecent texts that threaten morality and public decency'; the novel depicts sex and a gay character.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific PA confiscation; documented by ArabLit / English PEN.

entries.title: Chicago
entries.description_ban: Seized by Hamas police from Gaza City bookstores in January 2011 as 'immoral'/contrary to Sharia, with no court order. Authority: Hamas.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific Hamas seizure; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: Peace and Its Discontents
entries.description_ban: The Palestinian Authority under Arafat forbade circulation of Said's anti-Oslo essay collection in 1996. Authority: PA.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific PA circulation ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: A Banquet for Seaweed
entries.description_ban: Hamas police confiscated copies from Gaza City bookstores in January 2011 as 'against Sharia'. Authority: Hamas. Adds a Palestine ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Palestine (Hamas) ban; documented by Human Rights Watch.

entries.title: Speak, Bird, Speak Again
entries.description_ban: In 2007 the Hamas-run education ministry in Gaza ordered the Palestinian folktale collection pulled from school libraries over 'sexually explicit' content; the directive was reversed after public outcry. Authority: Hamas. Adds a Palestine ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Palestine (Hamas) ban; documented via Wikipedia (BBC/AP).

entries.title: A Banquet for Seaweed
entries.description_ban: Banned in Syria (and several Arab states) on publication; later condemned as blasphemous. Adds a Syria ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Syria ban; documented by Ahram Online (medium confidence).

entries.title: The Silence and the Roar
entries.description_ban: Dystopian novel about a writer crushed by a personality-cult dictatorship; banned in Syria, where Sirees's work had been suppressed since 1998, forcing publication in Beirut. Adds a Syria ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Syria ban; documented by Publishing Perspectives.
